title: GAP-DETECT output — blast-ranked gaps with interpretations and disposition

grounding_ref.description: path string if 03-grounding/ was read, else null

baseline_map_ref.description: brownfield only: path to baseline-map.json; null for greenfield

baseline_aprd_ref.description: brownfield only: path to aprd.frozen.md; null for greenfield

id.description: stable gap id; G* contiguous from G1, never renumbered

gap.description: one line: the ambiguity framed as a fork, not the answer; a place a competent engineer could build two different things

items.description: extraction id (R*, U*, E*) the gap directly arises from

refs.description: non-empty list of extraction ids the gap directly arises from

seam_ref.description: feature-add optional: {at:C*, contract_ref:CT*} when fork sits at an integration seam; null otherwise

contract_ref.description: contract id CT* at the seam

recommended_default.description: verbatim copy of one interpretation; the least-surprise reading adopted absent a client answer

disposition.description: ask iff blast_radius in {architecture, scope}; assume iff cosmetic — deterministic from blast_radius

reason.description: why the two builds diverge AND why this blast tier

dismissed_unknowns.description: every U* not cited in any gap's refs; may be [] only if every unknown fed a gap

reason.description: why this unknown is not a build-divergence gap

gap_counts.description: integer tallies; total == gaps.length; tier sums must match

total.description: total gap count; must equal gaps.length and architecture + scope + cosmetic

gap_counts.x-walk: total == gaps.length; architecture + scope + cosmetic == total
